# Window OS Experience

> A free, browser-based Windows 11 simulator built with React and TypeScript. Full desktop, taskbar, start menu, and 40+ built-in apps — no install required, runs entirely client-side with localStorage persistence.

## About

Window OS Experience recreates the look and feel of Windows 11 in the browser as a Progressive Web App (PWA). It includes window management (drag, resize, snap layouts, minimize, maximize), a Start Menu, taskbar, notification center, quick settings, lock screen, boot animation, command palette (Ctrl+K), and dark/light themes.

## Built-in Apps

### Productivity
- Notepad, Text Editor, Markdown Notes, Sticky Notes, To-Do List
- Spreadsheet (with SUM/AVG/MIN/MAX formulas and CSV export)
- Calendar (month/week/day views), Focus Timer, Clipboard Manager
- PDF Reader, Whiteboard

### Creative
- Paint (with undo/redo, shapes, export)
- Photos, Camera, Video Player, Voice Recorder
- Code Editor (themes, live preview, command palette)

### Utilities
- File Explorer (hybrid local + virtual filesystem)
- Calculator, Unit Converter, Color Picker, Dictionary
- QR Code Generator, Snipping Tool, Password Vault (XOR-encrypted)
- Terminal, System Monitor, Task Manager, Settings

### Information
- Maps (Leaflet-based with routing and weather)
- Weather, News, Stocks (with sparkline charts), Clock

### Communication
- Mail, Chat, Contacts, Browser

### Entertainment
- Music Player, Games (Snake, 2048, Minesweeper, Memory, Tic-Tac-Toe, Typing)
- Store, About This PC

## Tech Stack

- React 18, Vite 5, TypeScript 5, Tailwind CSS v3
- Zustand for state management
- localStorage for persistence (no backend)
- shadcn/ui components, Leaflet for maps
- PWA with manifest and offline-ready assets

## Keyboard Shortcuts

- `Win` / `Ctrl+Esc` — Open Start Menu
- `Ctrl+K` / `Win+K` — Command Palette
- `Win+L` — Lock screen
- `Win+D` — Show desktop
- `Win+Tab` — Task View
- `Win+Arrow` — Snap windows

## Pages

- [/](/ ): Main desktop OS experience — boot into the simulated Windows 11 environment.
